name: Dr Katayoun Shahandeh
title: Lecturer in Museum Studies
department: School of Arts & Department of History of Art and Archaeology
expertise_topics: Katayoun Shahandeh's academic interests encompass non-Western art
with a particular focus on Iran. Her work engages with feminist art histories

and strategies of display. She critically examines the intersections of gender and cultural identity in non-Western artistic contexts and investigates the lasting impact of colonial legacies on cultural expression and exhibition practices. Committed to addressing issues affecting underrepresented communities
her research seeks to amplify marginalized voices
expanding discourse on non-Western art and gender dynamics
research_interests: Katayoun Shahandeh's academic interests encompass non-Western art, history, and society, with a particular focus on Iran. Her work engages with feminist art histories, gender studies, postcolonial theory, and strategies of display. She critically examines the intersections of gender and cultural identity in non-Western artistic contexts and investigates the lasting impact of colonial legacies on cultural expression and exhibition practices. Committed to addressing issues affecting underrepresented communities, her research seeks to amplify marginalized voices, expanding discourse on non-Western art and gender dynamics.
